info.name: Vesicash API — Merchants Integration
info.description:
## Vesicash API —Integration Collection

**Base URL:** `https://api.vesicash.com`  
**API Version:** v1  
**Protocol:** HTTPS only (HTTP is blocked)

---

### Quick Start

1. Open the **Variables** tab on this collection and fill in:
    
    - x-api-key — your Vesicash private key (from dashboard)
        
    - Idempotency-Key — An idempotency key prevents the same request from being processed more than once.
        
    - `webhook_url` — your server endpoint that receives Vesicash events
        
2. Use **Test keys** for development (same base URL).  
    Switch to **Live keys** after KYC approval for production.
    
3. All requests return a standard JSON envelope:
    
    ``` json
          {
          "status": "success" | "error",
          "code": 200,
          "message": "Human-readable message",
          "data": { ... }
          }
    
     ```
    

---

### Authentication

Every request (except `GET /v1/decision/ip`) requires these two headers:

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| x-api-key | Your private API key — **keep server-side only** |
| `public-key` | Your public API key — safe for frontend use |

Requests made without these headers return `401 Unauthorized`.

---

### Folders in this Collection

| Folder | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| 🔑 Auth & Setup | Webhook secret generation |
| 🌍 Reference Data | Countries, banks, client IP lookup |
| 💳 Card Payin — Checkout | Vesicash-hosted payment page flow |
| 💳 Card Payin — Non-Checkout | Marchants-hosted card UI flow |
| 🏦 Bank Payout | Outbound disbursement to bank accounts |
| ↩ Refund | Reverse a completed payment |
| 📋 Payment Queries | List, inspect and verify transactions |
| 💰 Wallet | Balance and transaction history |

---

### Failure Reason Codes

| Code | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `ABANDONED_TRANSACTION` | PIN not entered in time |
| `DECLINED_TRANSACTION` | Rejected by processor |
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Insufficient wallet balance |
| `INVALID_PIN` | Authentication credential mismatch |
| `TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Exceeds configured threshold |
| `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` | Invalid account identifier |
| `ACCOUNT_BLOCKED` | Account suspended or locked |
| `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` | Third-party system delay |
| `TRANSACTION_FAILED` | General processor-side rejection |
| `TRANSACTION_PENDING` | Still processing at provider |

---

_Collection last updated: 2026-05-25_

item.name: 🔑 Auth & Setup

request.description:
## Verify API Credentials

> 🚧 **PLACEHOLDER** — Confirm the exact endpoint path with Vesicash backend. The request structure and response shape below follow standard API patterns.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/auth/verify`

Validates that your `x-api-key` is active and returns the associated account details. Run this first to confirm your integration is correctly configured before making any payment calls.

---

### Headers

| Header | Required | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `x-api-key` | ✅ | Your Vesicash secret API key |

---

### Success Response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "business_id": "biz_XXXXXXXXXX",
    "business_name": "Your Business Name",
    "environment": "live",
    "status": "active",
    "key_type": "secret"
  }
}
```

### Error Response (invalid key)

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "code": 401,
  "message": "Invalid API key"
}
```

---

> ✅ **Test keys** return `"environment": "test"`. Live keys return `"environment": "live"`. Never use test keys in production.

response.name: 401 — Invalid API Key

item.description:
## Auth & Setup

One-time setup calls to validate your credentials and configure webhook verification before going live.

**Prerequisites:**
1. Sign up at [vesicash.com](https://vesicash.com) and complete KYC
2. Go to **Dashboard → Developer → API Keys** to generate your `secret_key` and `public_key`
3. Add both keys to this collection's Variables tab
4. Run **Verify API Credentials** to confirm your keys are active
5. (Optional) Call **Generate Webhook Secret** and store the result securely on your server

---

### Authentication Headers

Every API request requires at least the `x-api-key` header:

| Header | Value | When required |
|--------|-------|---------------|
| `x-api-key` | Your secret API key | All requests |
| `public-key` | Your public API key | Customer-facing calls |
| `Idempotency-Key` | Any unique string (UUID recommended) | POST requests — prevents duplicate transactions |

item.name: 💳 Mobile money Payin — Checkout Flow

request.description:
## Initialize Payment — Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Creates a new card payment transaction. Vesicash returns a hosted `payment_link` to which you redirect the customer. Vesicash handles card data collection and 3DS2 entirely.

---

### Request Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | Transaction amount (in smallest currency unit, e.g. pesewas for GHS) |
| `country` | string | ✅ | ISO country code of the transaction (e.g. `GH`) |
| `currency` | string | ✅ | Currency code (e.g. `GHS`) |
| `method` | string | ✅ | Must be `"card"` for card payments |
| `operator` | string | ✅ | Must be `"mpgs"` (Mastercard Payment Gateway Services) |
| `narration` | string | ✅ | Description of the transaction (shown on receipt) |
| `redirect_success_url` | string | ✅ | URL Vesicash redirects to on successful payment |
| `redirect_failed_url` | string | ✅ | URL Vesicash redirects to on failed payment |
| `webhook_url` | string | ✅ | Your server endpoint for async status notifications |
| `integration_type` | string | ✅ | Must be `"DIRECT_API"` |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "message": "Payment initialized",
  "data": {
    "payment_link": "https://checkout.vesicash.com/pay/abc123",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "payment_id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending"
  }
}
```

---

### Next Step
Redirect the customer to `data.payment_link`. After payment, Vesicash will redirect back to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url`. **Always verify the outcome using `GET /v1/payment/:reference`.**

## Verify Payment Status (Post-Redirect)

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Call this after the customer returns to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url` — or upon receiving a webhook event — to confirm the authoritative payment outcome.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` returned by `POST /v1/payment/init`. Auto-populated from the previous step's test script. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "amount": 10000,
    "type": "payment",
    "method": "card",
    "status": "success",
    "currency": "GHS",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "business_id": "biz_XXX",
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
    "environment": "live",
    "redirect_url": "https://yourbank.com/payment/success",
    "email": "customer@email.com",
    "customer_first_name": "John",
    "customer_last_name": "Doe",
    "phone_number": "+233XXXXXXXXX"
  }
}
```

---

> ⚠️ **Critical:** Never finalise an order, credit a wallet, or trigger fulfilment based on the redirect URL params alone. Always call this endpoint to confirm `status: "success"` before taking business action.

item.description:
## Mobile money Payin — Checkout Flow

Vesicash hosts the payment page. The merchants redirects the customer to a `payment_link` where Vesicash customers details and handle the payment

**When to use:** When you want the simplest integration with zero PCI-DSS scope on card data — Vesicash handles the card UI entirely.

---

### Flow Summary

```
1. Merchants Backend  →  POST /api/v1/transactions/initiate  →  Vesicash
2. Vesicash       →  Returns payment_link + reference
3. Merchant          →  Redirect customer to payment_link
4. Customer      →  completes payment
5. Vesicash       →  Peoccess the payment
6. Vesicash       →  sends webhook
7. Merchant Backend  →  GET /api/v1/transactions/{{transaction_reference}}/status  (verify status — do NOT rely on redirect URL alone)

 ```

---

> ⚠️ Always verify final status via the API (step 8). Never confirm an order based on the redirect URL alone.

item.name: 💳 Mobile money Payin — Non-Checkout Flow

request.description:
## Initialize Payment — Non-Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Same endpoint as the checkout flow, but returns a `payment_reference` (not a `payment_link`) which you use in the next step to submit card details directly.

---

### Request Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | Amount in smallest unit (pesewas, kobo, etc.) |
| `country` | string | ✅ | ISO country code |
| `currency` | string | ✅ | Currency code |
| `method` | string | ✅ | `"card"` |
| `operator` | string | ✅ | `"mpgs"` |
| `narration` | string | ✅ | Transaction description |
| `redirect_success_url` | string | ✅ | Post-payment success redirect |
| `redirect_failed_url` | string | ✅ | Post-payment failure redirect |
| `webhook_url` | string | ✅ | Webhook notification endpoint |
| `integration_type` | string | ✅ | `"DIRECT_API"` |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "payment_id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending"
  }
}
```

**Note:** In the non-checkout flow, `data.reference` is the `payment_reference` you pass to the next step. There is no `payment_link`.

response.name: 1. Initialize Payment with minimum required fields

exec: console.log('[Vesicash] Bank FE must POST methodPostData to methodUrl.');
console.log('[Vesicash] Payment succeeded. Verify via GET /v1/payment/:ref');

request.description:
## Complete Payment — Submit Card Data

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/pay/:payment_reference`

Submit the customer's card details and device fingerprint to complete the payment initiated in the previous step. Vesicash processes the card through the MPGS network and triggers 3DS2 if required.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` from `POST /v1/payment/init`. Auto-populated. |

---

### Request Body

**Customer Identity:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `phone` | string | ✅ | Customer phone in international format |
| `email` | string | ✅ | Customer email address |
| `name` | string | ✅ | Customer full name |

**Card Details:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `card.number` | string | ✅ | PAN — 16-digit card number |
| `card.expiry` | string | ✅ | Expiry in `MM/YY` format |
| `card.cvv` | string | ✅ | 3 or 4-digit security code |

**Device Fingerprint (all required for 3DS2 risk scoring):**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `device.screen_width` | integer | Browser viewport width in pixels |
| `device.screen_height` | integer | Browser viewport height in pixels |
| `device.browser_agent` | string | Full `User-Agent` string |
| `device.user_ip` | string | Customer's public IP — use `GET /v1/decision/ip` from browser |
| `device.time_zone` | string | IANA timezone (e.g. `Africa/Accra`) |
| `device.java_enabled` | boolean | `navigator.javaEnabled()` |
| `device.language` | string | Browser language (e.g. `en-GB`) |

---

### 3DS2 Challenge Response

If the issuer requires a challenge, Vesicash returns:

```json
{
  "status": "challenge_required",
  "data": {
    "device_storage_token": "...",
    "methodUrl": "https://issuer-acs.example.com/3ds/method",
    "methodPostData": "<base64-encoded-payload>"
  }
}
```

**Your bank frontend must:**
1. POST `methodPostData` to `methodUrl` (Card Issuer ACS)
2. Display the issuer's challenge to the customer (OTP / in-app / biometric)
3. On completion, Vesicash will deliver the final result via webhook

---

### Success Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "status": "success",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX"
  }
}
```

## Verify Transaction Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Returns the full transaction record including all fields relevant for audit, refund eligibility and fraud monitoring.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` from the init step. Auto-populated. |

---

### Response (key fields)

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "amount": 10000,
    "charge": 150,
    "environment": "live",
    "type": "payment",
    "method": "card",
    "status": "success",
    "currency": "GHS",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "narration": "Payment for Order #12345",
    "provider_transaction_id": "MPGS-XXXXXX",
    "operator": "mpgs",
    "business_id": "biz_XXX",
    "is_disbursed": false,
    "is_fraud_resolved": false,
    "refunds": [],
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:05Z"
  }
}
```

---

### Fields to monitor

| Field | What to watch for |
|-------|------------------|
| `status` | `"success"` before confirming order |
| `is_fraud_resolved` | `false` = still under fraud review |
| `failure_reason` | Present on failed transactions |
| `refunds` | Array grows as refunds are processed |

item.description:
## Card Payin — Non-Checkout Flow

The Non‑Checkout Flow allows you to collect mobile money payments without redirecting the customer to the **Vesicash checkout page.**

**When to use:** This flow is ideal when you want full control of the payment experience within your website, mobile app, or custom UI.

### Flow Summary

```
1. Backend  →  POST /api/v1/transactions/initiate                 →  Vesicash
2. Vesicash       →  Returns payment_reference
3. Backend →  Complete payment
4. Backend  →  POST /v1/payment/pay/:reference         →  Vesicash
                    (customers details and Mobile money number)
5. Vesicash       →  POST webhook to webhook_url  (async)
7. Backend  →  GET /api/v1/transactions/{{transaction_reference}}/status  (verify status — do NOT rely on redirect URL alone)

 ```

---

item.name: 💳 Card Payin — Checkout Flow

## Initialize Payment — Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Creates a new card payment transaction. Vesicash returns a hosted `payment_link` to which you redirect the customer. Vesicash handles card data collection and 3DS2 entirely.

## Verify Payment Status (Post-Redirect)

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Call this after the customer returns to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url` — or upon receiving a webhook event — to confirm the authoritative payment outcome.

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |

### Response

item.description:
## Card Payin — Checkout Flow

Vesicash hosts the payment page. The merchant redirects the customer to a `payment_link` where Vesicash collects card details and handles the full 3DS2 flow.

**When to use:** When you want the simplest integration with zero PCI-DSS scope on card data — Vesicash handles the card UI entirely.

---

### Flow Summary

```
1. Backend  →  POST /api/v1/transactions/initiate  →  Vesicash
2. Vesicash       →  Returns payment_link + reference
3. Backend          →  Redirect customer to payment_link
4. Customer      →  Enters card on Vesicash hosted page
5. Vesicash       →  3DS2 auth with Card Issuer (MPGS)
6. Vesicash       →  Redirects to redirect_success_url or redirect_failed_url
7. Vesicash       →  POST webhook event to webhook_url (async)
8. Backend  →  GET /api/v1/transactions/{{transaction_reference}}/status (verify status — do NOT rely on redirect URL alone)

 ```

---

> ⚠️ Always verify final status via the API (step 8). Never confirm an order based on the redirect URL alone.

item.name: 💳 Card Payin — Non-Checkout Flow

request.description:
## Initialize Payment — Non-Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Same endpoint as the checkout flow, but returns a `payment_reference` (not a `payment_link`) which you use in the next step to submit card details directly.

---

### Request Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | Amount in smallest unit (pesewas, kobo, etc.) |
| `country` | string | ✅ | ISO country code |
| `currency` | string | ✅ | Currency code |
| `method` | string | ✅ | `"card"` |
| `operator` | string | ✅ | `"mpgs"` |
| `narration` | string | ✅ | Transaction description |
| `redirect_success_url` | string | ✅ | Post-payment success redirect |
| `redirect_failed_url` | string | ✅ | Post-payment failure redirect |
| `webhook_url` | string | ✅ | Webhook notification endpoint |
| `integration_type` | string | ✅ | `"DIRECT_API"` |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "payment_id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending"
  }
}
```

**Note:** In the non-checkout flow, `data.reference` is the `payment_reference` you pass to the next step. There is no `payment_link`.

console.log('[Vesicash] Payment succeeded. Verify via GET /v1/payment/:ref');

## Complete Payment — Submit Card Data

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/pay/:payment_reference`

Submit the customer's card details and device fingerprint to complete the payment initiated in the previous step. Vesicash processes the card through the MPGS network and triggers 3DS2 if required.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` from `POST /v1/payment/init`. Auto-populated. |

---

### Request Body

**Customer Identity:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `phone` | string | ✅ | Customer phone in international format |
| `email` | string | ✅ | Customer email address |
| `name` | string | ✅ | Customer full name |

**Card Details:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `card.number` | string | ✅ | PAN — 16-digit card number |
| `card.expiry` | string | ✅ | Expiry in `MM/YY` format |
| `card.cvv` | string | ✅ | 3 or 4-digit security code |

**Device Fingerprint (all required for 3DS2 risk scoring):**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `device.screen_width` | integer | Browser viewport width in pixels |
| `device.screen_height` | integer | Browser viewport height in pixels |
| `device.browser_agent` | string | Full `User-Agent` string |
| `device.user_ip` | string | Customer's public IP — use `GET /v1/decision/ip` from browser |
| `device.time_zone` | string | IANA timezone (e.g. `Africa/Accra`) |
| `device.java_enabled` | boolean | `navigator.javaEnabled()` |
| `device.language` | string | Browser language (e.g. `en-GB`) |

---

### 3DS2 Challenge Response

If the issuer requires a challenge, Vesicash returns:

```json
{
  "status": "challenge_required",
  "data": {
    "device_storage_token": "...",
    "methodUrl": "https://issuer-acs.example.com/3ds/method",
    "methodPostData": "<base64-encoded-payload>"
  }
}
```

**Your bank frontend must:**
1. POST `methodPostData` to `methodUrl` (Card Issuer ACS)
2. Display the issuer's challenge to the customer (OTP / in-app / biometric)
3. On completion, Vesicash will deliver the final result via webhook

---

### Success Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "status": "success",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX"
  }
}
```

## Verify Transaction Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Returns the full transaction record including all fields relevant for audit, refund eligibility and fraud monitoring.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` from the init step. Auto-populated. |

---

### Response (key fields)

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "amount": 10000,
    "charge": 150,
    "environment": "live",
    "type": "payment",
    "method": "card",
    "status": "success",
    "currency": "GHS",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "narration": "Payment for Order #12345",
    "provider_transaction_id": "MPGS-XXXXXX",
    "operator": "mpgs",
    "business_id": "biz_XXX",
    "is_disbursed": false,
    "is_fraud_resolved": false,
    "refunds": [],
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:05Z"
  }
}
```

---

### Fields to monitor

| Field | What to watch for |
|-------|------------------|
| `status` | `"success"` before confirming order |
| `is_fraud_resolved` | `false` = still under fraud review |
| `failure_reason` | Present on failed transactions |
| `refunds` | Array grows as refunds are processed |

item.description:
## Card Payin — Non-Checkout Flow

The bank hosts its own card data collection UI. Card details are submitted directly to Vesicash via the backend. The bank is responsible for handling the 3DS2 challenge redirect.

**When to use:** When you want full control over the payment UX and are willing to manage 3DS2 redirect handling.

---

### Flow Summary

```
1. Bank Backend  →  POST /v1/payment/init                  →  Vesicash
2. Vesicash       →  Returns payment_reference
3. Bank Frontend →  Customer enters card details on bank's UI
4. Bank Backend  →  POST /v1/payment/pay/:reference         →  Vesicash
                    (card data + device fingerprint)
5a. Vesicash      →  If 3DS required: returns challenge_required
                    + methodUrl (Card Issuer ACS URL)
                    + methodPostData (encoded payload)
5b. Bank Frontend→  POSTs methodPostData to methodUrl
5c. Card Issuer  →  Customer completes OTP / biometric
5d. Vesicash      →  3DS auth complete
6. Vesicash       →  POST webhook to webhook_url  (async)
7. Bank Backend  →  GET /v1/payment/:ref  (verify final status)
```

---

> 📌 **Device Fingerprint:** Call `GET /v1/decision/ip` from the customer's browser to get their public IP. Collect the other device fields (`screen_width`, `browser_agent`, etc.) client-side.

item.name: 🏦 Mobile Money Payout — Disbursement

request.description:
## Check Wallet Balance

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/merchant/wallets`

Returns all active wallets for your Vesicash account. **Always check your wallet balance before initiating a payout** — insufficient funds will cause the payout to fail immediately with `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`.

---

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `uuid` | string | Wallet unique identifier |
| `country_code` | string | ISO country code of this wallet |
| `currency` | string | Currency code (e.g. `ZMW`, `GHS`) |
| `balance` | number | Available payout balance |
| `collection_balance` | number | Collected funds not yet settled |
| `status` | string | `"active"` or `"suspended"` |

---

> ✅ Ensure `balance` ≥ the payout amount before proceeding to Step 2.

exec: console.log('[Vesicash] Wait for webhook or poll GET /v1/payment/' + json.data.reference);

request.description:
## Process Bank Payout

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/payouts/process`

Initiates a disbursement from your Vesicash wallet to a beneficiary's bank account.

---

### Request Body

**Required fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | integer | Payout amount in smallest currency unit. Must be a positive integer. |
| `countryId` | string | Country identifier (use `id` from `GET /v1/countries`) |
| `transfer_to` | string | Must be `"bank"` for bank account payouts |
| `bank_id` | string | Bank identifier from `GET /v1/payment/banks` |
| `account_number` | string | Beneficiary's bank account number |

**Optional fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `narration` | string | Description shown on bank statement |
| `webhook_url` | string | Endpoint to receive payout status notifications |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 0,
  "message": "Payout request accepted",
  "data": {
    "reference": "APX-PO-XXXXXX",
    "amount": 50000,
    "status": "processing"
  }
}
```

---

### Failure Codes Specific to Payouts

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Wallet balance too low — check with `GET /v1/account/get-wallet` |
| `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` | Invalid `account_number` or `bank_id` combination |
| `ACCOUNT_BLOCKED` | Beneficiary account is frozen |
| `TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Amount exceeds configured payout limit |

## Check Payout Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Verifies the final outcome of a payout. Call after receiving a webhook notification, or poll periodically if webhooks are not configured.

---

### Key Response Fields for Payouts

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `status` | `"success"`, `"failed"`, or `"processing"` |
| `is_disbursed` | `true` when funds have been delivered to the beneficiary |
| `failure_reason` | Present if `status` is `"failed"` |
| `amount` | Disbursed amount |
| `reference` | Your payout reference |
| `created_at` | When the payout was initiated |
| `updated_at` | Last status change timestamp |

item.description:
## Mobile Money Payout — Disbursement Flow

Disburse funds from your Vesicash wallet to a beneficiary's mobile money account.

**Prerequisites:**
- Your Vesicash wallet must have sufficient balance (Step 1)
- The beneficiary's mobile money number must be active

---

### Flow Summary

```
1. Backend  →  GET  /api/v1/merchant/wallets          (confirm sufficient balance)
2. Backend  →  POST /api/v1/transactions/pay-out      (initiate payout)
3. Vesicash →  Routes funds via mobile money operator
4. Vesicash →  POST webhook: pending → completed | failed
5. Backend  →  GET  /api/v1/transactions/:ref/status  (verify final status)
```

---

### Payout Status Lifecycle

`pending` → `completed` | `failed`

item.name: 🏦 Bank Payout — Disbursement

request.description:

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/merchant/wallets`

Returns all active wallets for your Vesicash account. **Always check your wallet balance before initiating a payout** — insufficient funds will cause the payout to fail immediately with `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`.

---

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `uuid` | string | Wallet unique identifier |
| `country_code` | string | ISO country code of this wallet |
| `currency` | string | Currency code (e.g. `GHS`, `NGN`) |
| `balance` | number | Available payout balance |
| `collection_balance` | number | Collected funds not yet settled |
| `status` | string | `"active"` or `"suspended"` |

---

> ✅ Ensure `balance` ≥ the payout amount before proceeding to Step 2.

## Process Bank Payout

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/payouts/process`

Initiates a disbursement from your Vesicash wallet to a beneficiary's bank account.

---

### Request Body

**Required fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | integer | Payout amount in smallest currency unit. Must be a positive integer. |
| `countryId` | string | Country identifier (use `id` from `GET /v1/countries`) |
| `transfer_to` | string | Must be `"bank"` for bank account payouts |
| `bank_id` | string | Bank identifier from `GET /v1/payment/banks` |
| `account_number` | string | Beneficiary's bank account number |

**Optional fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `narration` | string | Description shown on bank statement |
| `webhook_url` | string | Endpoint to receive payout status notifications |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 0,
  "message": "Payout request accepted",
  "data": {
    "reference": "APX-PO-XXXXXX",
    "amount": 50000,
    "status": "processing"
  }
}
```

---

### Failure Codes Specific to Payouts

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Wallet balance too low — check with `GET /v1/account/get-wallet` |
| `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` | Invalid `account_number` or `bank_id` combination |
| `ACCOUNT_BLOCKED` | Beneficiary account is frozen |
| `TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Amount exceeds configured payout limit |

## Check Payout Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Verifies the final outcome of a payout. Call after receiving a webhook notification, or poll periodically if webhooks are not configured.

---

### Key Response Fields for Payouts

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `status` | `"success"`, `"failed"`, or `"processing"` |
| `is_disbursed` | `true` when funds have been delivered to the beneficiary |
| `failure_reason` | Present if `status` is `"failed"` |
| `amount` | Disbursed amount |
| `reference` | Your payout reference |
| `created_at` | When the payout was initiated |
| `updated_at` | Last status change timestamp |

item.description:
## Bank Payout — Disbursement Flow

Disburse funds from your Vesicash wallet to a beneficiary's bank account.

**Prerequisites:**
- Your Vesicash wallet must have sufficient balance (Step 1)
- You must know the beneficiary's bank code and account number

---

### Flow Summary

```
1. Backend  →  GET  /api/v1/merchant/wallets          (confirm sufficient balance)
2. Backend  →  POST /api/v1/transactions/pay-out      (initiate bank payout)
3. Vesicash →  Routes funds via banking network
4. Vesicash →  POST webhook: pending → completed | failed
5. Backend  →  GET  /api/v1/transactions/:ref/status  (verify final status)
```

---

### Payout Status Lifecycle

`pending` → `completed` | `failed`

item.name: ↩ Refund

exec: console.log('[Vesicash] Status: pending — wait for webhook or poll status.');

request.description:
## Process Refund

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/refunds/process`

Initiates a refund against a completed card payment. Vesicash queues the reversal and routes it through the card network back to the customer's card issuer.

---

### Request Body

**Required fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | Refund amount — must be ≤ original payment amount |
| `payment_reference` | string | The `reference` from the original successful payment |
| `reason` | string | Human-readable reason for the refund (stored for audit) |
| `country_id` | string | Country identifier (use `id` from `GET /v1/countries`) |

**Optional fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `webhook_url` | string | Endpoint to receive refund status notifications |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "message": "Refund queued",
  "data": {
    "refund_id": "ref_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending",
    "reference": "APX-RF-XXXXXX"
  }
}
```

---

### Validation Rules
- Vesicash returns `400` if `amount` exceeds the original payment
- The `payment_reference` must map to a completed (not failed) transaction
- Multiple partial refunds are supported up to the original amount total

---

### Failure Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Wallet balance insufficient to fund the refund |
| `DECLINED_TRANSACTION` | Refund rejected by card network |
| `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` | Third-party system delay — retry may be appropriate |

exec: console.log('[Vesicash] Refund', i + 1, ':', r.refund_id, '|', r.status, '| Amount:', r.amount);

request.description:
## Check Refund Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Returns the full transaction record including the `refunds[]` array. Use this to confirm the refund outcome after receiving a webhook notification.

---

### Key Fields to Inspect

```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "success",
    "refunds": [
      {
        "refund_id": "ref_XXXXXX",
        "amount": 10000,
        "status": "success",
        "reason": "Customer requested cancellation",
        "reference": "APX-RF-XXXXXX",
        "created_at": "2026-05-25T11:00:00Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-05-25T11:00:30Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

### Refund Status Values

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `pending` | Queued, not yet processed |
| `success` | Funds reversed to cardholder |
| `failed` | Reversal failed — see `failure_reason` |

item.description:
## Refund Flow

Reverse a completed card and mobile money payment back to the customer.

**Prerequisites:**

- A `payment_reference` for a **successfully completed** card transaction must exist
    
- Refund amount must not exceed the original payment amount
    
- Partial refunds are supported (refund less than the original)
    

---

### Flow Summary

```
1. Bank Backend  →  POST /api/v1/transactions/:ransaction_reference/refund
2. Vesicash       →  Queues refund, returns { refund_id, status: "pending" }
3. Vesicash       →  Routes reversal through MPGS to card issuer or the mobile money
4. Vesicash       →  POST webhook: pending → success | failed
5. Backend  →  GET /api/v1/transactions/{{transaction_reference}}/status

 ```

item.name: 📋 Payment Queries

query.description: Start of date range as Unix timestamp (optional).
End of date range as Unix timestamp (optional).

request.description:
## Get All Payments for Business

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment`

Returns a paginated list of all transactions associated with your business account.

---

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | No | Page number. Default: `1` |
| `limit` | integer | No | Results per page |
| `reference` | string | No | Filter to a specific transaction reference |
| `from` | integer | No | Start of range — Unix timestamp |
| `to` | integer | No | End of range — Unix timestamp |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "payments": [
      {
        "id": "pay_XXX",
        "amount": 10000,
        "environment": "live",
        "type": "payment",
        "method": "card",
        "status": "success",
        "currency": "GHS",
        "reference": "APX-REF-XXX",
        "narration": "Payment for Order #12345",
        "provider_transaction_id": "MPGS-XXX",
        "business_id": "biz_XXX",
        "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:05Z"
      }
    ],
    "page": 1,
    "pageCount": 5,
    "totalPages": 5
  }
}
```

## Get Payment Status by Reference

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Fetches the current state of a single payment. Optimised for post-redirect and post-webhook status confirmation.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` returned at payment initialisation. Auto-populated from collection variable. |

---

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | string | Internal payment ID |
| `amount` | number | Transaction amount |
| `type` | string | `"payment"`, `"payout"`, `"refund"` |
| `method` | string | `"card"` |
| `status` | string | `"success"`, `"failed"`, `"pending"`, `"abandoned"` |
| `currency` | string | e.g. `"GHS"` |
| `reference` | string | Your transaction reference |
| `business_id` | string | Your Vesicash business account ID |
| `created_at` | string | ISO 8601 timestamp |
| `environment` | string | `"live"` or `"test"` |
| `redirect_url` | string | The redirect URL used |
| `email` | string | Customer email |
| `customer_first_name` | string | Customer first name |
| `customer_last_name` | string | Customer last name |
| `phone_number` | string | Customer phone |

## Get Full Transaction Details

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Returns the complete transaction record, including fields specific to refunds, payouts, and fraud monitoring. Use this endpoint when you need the full audit trail.

---

### Additional Fields (vs. basic status check)

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `charge` | number | Vesicash processing fee applied |
| `narration` | string | Transaction description |
| `provider_transaction_id` | string | ID assigned by MPGS / banking network |
| `operator` | string | Payment network used (e.g. `"mpgs"`) |
| `is_disbursed` | boolean | `true` when payout funds have reached beneficiary |
| `is_fraud_resolved` | boolean | `false` while fraud investigation is open |
| `refunds` | array | All refunds associated with this transaction |
| `updated_at` | string | Last status change timestamp |

---

### Use Cases
- Dispute resolution: check `is_fraud_resolved` and `refunds[]`
- Payout audit: check `is_disbursed`
- Reconciliation: compare `amount`, `charge`, `provider_transaction_id`

item.description:
Query and inspect transactions across all flow types (card payments, payouts, refunds).

| Endpoint | Use for |
| --- | --- |
| `GET` /api/v1/transactions | Paginated list of all business transactions |
| `GET` /api/v1/transactions/{{transaction_reference}}/status | Payment summary — customer details, status, timestamps |

item.name: 💰 Wallet

request.description:
## Get Wallet by Country

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/wallet/:country_code`

Returns the single wallet for the specified country. Use the `country_code` collection variable (default: `GH`) or pass a specific ISO code.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `country_code` | string | ✅ | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (e.g. `GH`, `ZM`, `NG`, `KE`) |

---

### Headers

| Header | Required | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `x-api-key` | ✅ | Your Vesicash secret key |

## Get All Wallets

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/merchant/wallets`

Returns all wallets associated with your Vesicash merchant account, one per supported country. Use this to check balances before initiating payouts.

---

### Headers

| Header | Required | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `x-api-key` | ✅ | Your Vesicash secret key |

---

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `uuid` | string | Wallet unique identifier |
| `country_code` | string | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code |
| `currency` | string | Wallet currency (e.g. `GHS`, `ZMW`, `NGN`) |
| `balance` | number | Available balance for payouts |
| `collection_balance` | number | Collected (inbound) funds pending settlement |
| `status` | string | `"active"` — wallet is operational |

item.description:
## Wallet

Query your Vesicash wallet balances across supported markets.

Your Vesicash account has one wallet per supported country. Each wallet has:
- A **balance** — funds available for payouts and refunds
- A **collection_balance** — inbound payments not yet settled to your balance

---

### Supported Markets

| Country | Currency Code |
|---------|---------------|
| Ghana | GHS |
| Zambia | ZMW |
| Nigeria | NGN |
| Kenya | KES |
| Tanzania | TZS |
| United States | USD |

item.name: 🔔 Webhooks
item.description:
## Webhooks

Vesicash delivers real-time event notifications to your server via HTTP POST requests. Configure your webhook URL in the **Auth & Setup** folder or on your Vesicash dashboard.

---

### Webhook Security

Every webhook request includes an `x-vesicash-signature` header containing an HMAC-SHA256 signature of the raw request body, signed with your webhook secret.

Always verify this signature before processing any event:

```javascript
const crypto = require('crypto');
const sig = req.headers['x-vesicash-signature'];
const expected = crypto.createHmac('sha256', WEBHOOK_SECRET).update(rawBody).digest('hex');
if (!crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(expected), Buffer.from(sig))) {
  return res.status(400).send('Invalid signature');
}
```

---

### Retry Policy

If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status, Vesicash will retry with exponential backoff:
- 1st retry: 1 minute
- 2nd retry: 5 minutes
- 3rd retry: 30 minutes
- 4th retry: 2 hours
- 5th retry: 6 hours

After 5 failed attempts, the event is marked as undelivered. Contact support to replay missed events.

---

### Event Types

| Event | Trigger |
|-------|---------|
| `payment.completed` | Customer payment successfully processed |
| `payment.failed` | Payment attempt failed |
| `payout.completed` | Payout delivered to beneficiary |
| `payout.failed` | Payout could not be delivered |
| `refund.completed` | Refund successfully reversed to customer |
| `refund.failed` | Refund could not be processed |

header.description: HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body, signed with your webhook secret. Always verify before processing.

request.description:
## Webhook: payment.completed

> 🚧 **PLACEHOLDER** — This request simulates the webhook payload Vesicash sends to your `webhook_url` when a payment is successfully completed. Set `webhook_url` in collection variables to your local test server (use a tool like ngrok or webhook.site during development).

---

Triggered when a customer payment is fully processed and funds are available in your collection balance.

### Payload Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `event` | string | Always `"payment.completed"` |
| `data.reference` | string | Vesicash transaction reference |
| `data.merchant_reference` | string | Your custom reference (if provided at init) |
| `data.status` | string | Always `"completed"` for this event |
| `data.amount` | number | Amount collected |
| `data.currency` | string | Currency of the transaction |
| `data.net_amount` | number | Amount after Vesicash fees |

---

### Your webhook handler should:
1. Verify `x-vesicash-signature` before any processing
2. Respond with `200 OK` immediately (process async)
3. Look up the order by `data.merchant_reference`
4. Confirm `data.status === "completed"` before fulfilling the order
5. Mark the order as paid and trigger fulfillment

## Webhook: payment.failed

> 🚧 **PLACEHOLDER** — This request simulates the webhook Vesicash sends when a payment attempt fails.

---

Triggered when a payment cannot be processed — due to insufficient funds, network timeout, or provider rejection.

### Key Fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `data.status` | Always `"failed"` |
| `data.failure_reason` | Machine-readable failure code (see Error Reference) |

### Your handler should:
1. Verify signature
2. Notify the customer of the failed payment
3. Do NOT fulfil the order
4. Optionally prompt retry

## Webhook: payout.completed

> 🚧 **PLACEHOLDER** — Simulates the webhook sent when a disbursement is successfully delivered.

---

`is_disbursed: true` confirms funds have reached the beneficiary. Update your records accordingly.

## Webhook: payout.failed

> 🚧 **PLACEHOLDER** — Simulates the webhook sent when a disbursement fails.

---

Check `failure_reason` to determine the cause. Common reasons: `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND`, `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`, `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT`.

## Webhook: refund.completed

> 🚧 **PLACEHOLDER** — Simulates the webhook sent when a refund is successfully reversed to the customer.

---

Update your order management system to reflect the refund. Check `original_payment_reference` to match against the original order.

exec: // Collection-level pre-request: validate keys are set
console.warn('[Vesicash] secret_key is not set. Set it in the collection variables.');
console.warn('[Vesicash] public_key is not set. Set it in the collection variables.');

variable.description: Vesicash API base URL. Use this for both test and live environments — differentiated by key type only.
Your Vesicash private (secret) key. Keep this server-side only. Generate from the Developer tab in your Vesicash dashboard settings.
Your Vesicash public key. Safe to use from frontend integrations. Generate from the Developer tab in your Vesicash dashboard settings.
Your server endpoint URL that will receive Vesicash webhook event POSTs. Must be publicly reachable over HTTPS.
Auto-populated by the 'Initialize Payment' request test script. Used by downstream requests in the non-checkout flow.
Auto-populated by the 'Initialize Payment' request test script. The Vesicash internal payment ID.
Auto-populated by the 'Process Refund' request test script.
Auto-populated by the 'Generate Webhook Secret' request test script. Used to verify HMAC-SHA256 signatures on incoming webhooks.
ISO country code used for bank lookups, payouts and refunds. Default: GH (Ghana). Change to match your operating country.
Auto-populated by Initialize Payment test scripts. The Vesicash transaction reference (e.g. PAYIN-JP83-XXXXXXXXXX). Used in all status-check and complete-payment requests.
Customer's mobile money phone number in international format (e.g. 233244000000 for Ghana MTN). Required for mobile money payin and payout requests.
Beneficiary bank account number for bank payouts.
Beneficiary account holder name for bank payouts.
Bank code for the beneficiary institution (e.g. '006' for UBA Ghana).
Human-readable bank name for the beneficiary institution.
